skill.name: stream-chain
skill.description: Build sequential Claude Flow workflows where each step uses the previous output as context.
skill.summary: Chain multi-step AI workflows for analysis, refactoring, testing, optimization, and data transformation.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 93 backtick execution detections and 15 weak cryptography blockers in SKILL.md. Review found no Ruby execution, cryptographic implementation, network exfiltration, or prompt injection; those alerts are Markdown examples and keyword matches. The remaining risks are user-directed CLI execution and documented local persistence of chain outputs.

medium_findings.title: User-Directed CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill documents many claude-flow shell commands for running chains, pipelines, swarm coordination, and local inspection. This is expected for this workflow skill, but users should review commands before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible shell examples, but the file is documentation-only and does not execute them itself. Risk depends on user approval and the installed claude-flow behavior.

medium_findings.title: Local Persistence of Chain Outputs
medium_findings.description: The skill states that stream-chain results are stored under .claude-flow/memory/stream-chain/. This may persist sensitive code, analysis output, or user data on disk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly states that results are stored in a local memory directory. No exfiltration is shown, but persistence is a real privacy and data retention concern.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Detections Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The static backtick findings are Markdown inline code and fenced examples, not Ruby backtick execution. No Ruby code or shell substitution appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown documentation and code fences. There is no executable Ruby source file or command substitution construct.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography blockers appear to match words such as chain, design, critical, and pipeline text. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose, table rows, or prompt text rather than hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or key handling code.

content.user_title: Chain AI Workflows with Streamed Context
content.value_statement: Complex AI tasks often lose context between steps. Stream-Chain passes each result into the next step so teams can build repeatable analysis, refactoring, testing, and reporting workflows.
actual_capabilities: Runs custom prompt chains with two or more sequential steps.
Executes predefined analysis, refactor, test, and optimize pipelines.
Passes previous step output into the next prompt for context continuity.
Supports timeout, verbose, and debug options for pipeline runs.
Supports workflow patterns for code review, migration, and documentation tasks.
limitations: Requires a working claude-flow CLI installation outside this skill.
Does not include implementation source code for the stream-chain command.
Workflow quality depends on clear prompts and suitable step ordering.
May persist chain context locally through Claude Flow memory features.

use_cases.title: Codebase Onboarding
use_cases.description: Run analysis steps that map structure, find risks, and produce a practical onboarding report.

use_cases.title: Refactoring Planning
use_cases.description: Chain review, prioritization, implementation guidance, and verification into one repeatable workflow.

use_cases.title: Quality Review Automation
use_cases.description: Generate test plans, coverage notes, and optimization recommendations from earlier analysis output.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Analysis Chain
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for understanding a repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stream-chain to analyze this project, identify improvement areas, and produce an action plan.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Test Generation Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stream-chain to find untested areas, design test cases, and summarize the tests that should be added first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Refactor with Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for controlled code improvement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stream-chain to identify refactor candidates, rank them by risk, propose changes, and define verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Multi-Agent Delivery Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for multi-step feature delivery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stream-chain to research requirements, design an API, plan implementation, create tests, and prepare deployment notes.

output_examples.input: Analyze a new codebase and recommend next steps.
output: A structure summary that names major components.
A ranked list of improvement areas with rationale.
A concise action plan for follow-up work.

output_examples.input: Run a refactoring workflow for legacy modules.
output: A prioritized list of refactor candidates.
Verification notes for behavior and regression checks.

output_examples.input: Create a testing workflow for a feature branch.
output: Coverage gaps grouped by feature area.
Suggested unit and integration test scenarios.
A final quality report for review.

best_practices: Use specific prompts that name files, goals, and expected output format.
Place analysis before implementation so later steps use grounded context.
Add verification or review as the final step in every chain.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad commands without reviewing the generated plan first.
Do not put secrets or regulated data into prompts without retention controls.
Do not use vague prompts such as check security for complex audits.

faq.question: What does Stream-Chain do?
faq.answer: It runs sequential Claude Flow tasks where each step receives output from the previous step.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation for using claude-flow stream-chain commands.

faq.question: Which pipelines are documented?
faq.answer: The skill documents analysis, refactor, test, and optimize pipelines.

faq.question: Can I define custom pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes custom pipeline definitions in .claude-flow/config.json.

faq.question: Does it store workflow output?
faq.answer: The documentation says Claude Flow memory can store stream-chain results locally.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning about user-approved CLI commands and local output persistence.
